name: Autorun Keys Modification Detected
description: Detects modifications (creation, deletion, or setting values) in critical Windows registry keys that control auto-execution of programs at system boot or user logon. Adversaries commonly abuse these keys, such as `Run`, `RunOnce`, `Winlogon`, and `AppInit_DLLs`, to establish persistence.

prompts.prompt: A critical persistence mechanism has been detected on the host **{computer_name}**. The process **{image}**, running under user **{user}**, modified a critical Autorun registry key at path **{target_object}**. This registry modification is a known technique used by adversaries to establish persistence (**MITRE ATT&CK T1547.001**), ensuring their malicious code executes automatically upon system boot or user logon. The event details show the new value **{details}**. Immediate investigation is required to determine the legitimacy of this change. If the modification is not from a known, trusted application installer or update, this is a confirmed security incident that requires a high-priority response.

parameters.name: image
parameters.description: The process that performed the registry modification.

parameters.name: target_object
parameters.description: The full path of the registry key or value that was modified.

parameters.name: details
parameters.description: The new value written to the registry key, containing the program path.

parameters.name: user
parameters.description: The user account that performed the modification.

parameters.name: computer_name
parameters.description: The name of the computer where the event occurred.
